mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator simplifies the deployment and management of RabbitMQ clusters on Kubernetes. It automates tasks such as cluster creation, upgrades, ang creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. This operator is ideal for users looking to automate RabbitMQ deployments in Kubernetes environments.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://rabbitmq.github.io/cluster-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the RabbitMQ operator repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of RabbitMQ clusters.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --namespace rabbitmq --create-namespace --version 2.20.1

```

> Installs the operator in the 'rabbitmq' namespace.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> Deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters --namespace rabbitmq

```

> Lists all pods in the 'rabbitmq' namespace to verify their status.
> Checks the status of RabbitMQ clusters in the 'rabbitmq' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if not handled properly.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq

```

> Removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your RabbitMQ data. This step upgrades the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters --namespace rabbitmq -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --namespace rabbitmq --version 2.20.1

kubectl get pods --namespace rabbitmq

```

> Backup current RabbitMQ cluster configurations.
> Upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rabbitmqclusters --namespace rabbitmq -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RabbitMQ cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RabbitMQ cluster may not have sufficient resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Kubernetes nodes have enough CPU and memory resources available.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RabbitMQ cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RabbitMQ pods may be in a crash loop due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the RabbitMQ pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace rabbitmq' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RabbitMQ service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure it is correctly set up to expose the RabbitMQ ports.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition for RabbitMQ may not have been installed.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD by running 'kubectl apply -f https://github.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/releases/latest/download/cluster-operator.yml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for RabbitMQ 4.1.3, improved error handling during cluster creation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods --namespace rabbitmq'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
